title: Unlicensed firearms confiscated from Hartenbos, two arrested
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS -  The Da Gamaskop Police have arrested two men, one a 77-year-old, in Hartenbos yesterday, 20 January, for the possession of unlicensed firearms.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Da Gamaskop Police have arrested two men, one a 77-year-old, in Hartenbos yesterday, 20 January, for the possession of unlicensed firearms."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The Southern Cape Police’s spokesperson, Warrant Officer Chris Spies, said the officers had received some information about illegal activities at a premises in Hartenbos."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"They found a man in possession of an unlicensed .22 rifle, which they confiscated. The man was arrested on a charge of unlawful possession of a firearm.","html":"They found a man in possession of an unlicensed .22 rifle, which they confiscated. The man was arrested on a charge of unlawful possession of a firearm."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Further investigations led the police to a property in Hendrik Herbst Street, Hartenbos, later that evening.","html":"Further investigations led the police to a property in Hendrik Herbst Street, Hartenbos, later that evening."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“A search of the premises led to the discovery of a shotgun, a firearm barrel and two rounds of ammunition,” said Spies.","html":"“A search of the premises led to the discovery of a shotgun, a firearm barrel and two rounds of ammunition,” said Spies."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The members confiscated the items and arrested a 77-year-old man on charges of unlawful possession of a firearm and ammunition.”","html":"“The members confiscated the items and arrested a 77-year-old man on charges of unlawful possession of a firearm and ammunition.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Both men remain in police custody and are expected to make their first court appearance in the Mossel Bay Magistrate’s Court tomorrow, 22 January.","html":"Both men remain in police custody and are expected to make their first court appearance in the Mossel Bay Magistrate’s Court tomorrow, 22 January."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“These confiscations are in line with the policing priorities of the Western Cape, to rid communities of unlicensed firearms that threaten the safety of the public,” Spies said.","html":"“These confiscations are in line with the policing priorities of the Western Cape, to rid communities of unlicensed firearms that threaten the safety of the public,” Spies said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Unlicensed firearms confiscated from Hartenbos, two arrested
